comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Already implemented, reviewed via Gerrit change #321 and merged to main; plan review not applicable to completed+landed work".

compiled_state.description:
## What
Add a deterministic precheck to the completion-verification close gate: when a ticket
records `file_impact` (a claimed code change) but no commit references it via a
`rebar-ticket:` trailer, fail fast BEFORE the billable LLM verifier runs, with a message
describing the reason and remediation.

## Why
A ticket recording file_impact claims a concrete code change. If no commit references it,
the implementation has not landed and completion cannot be confirmed — so there is no point
spending an LLM call. This mirrors the existing open-children structural guard.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`: new helper `_referencing_commit_exists()` (scans
  `git log`, resolves each commit's `rebar-ticket:`/`<id>:` refs through the shared resolver);
  the precheck wired into `_completion_precheck` after the bug-reason check, before the LLM call.
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py`: coverage for block/pass/skip/force-close.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A ticket with file_impact and no referencing commit is blocked (exit 1) before the LLM call.
- [ ] The same ticket closes once a commit references it via a rebar-ticket trailer.
- [ ] A ticket without file_impact is unaffected (no commit check).
- [ ] `--force-close` bypasses the precheck (withholds signature, still closes).
- [ ] The error message names the reason (file_impact) and remediation (referencing commit).
compiled_state.title: Fail completion verification fast when a file_impact ticket has no referencing commit
